observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require (a) the concerned responsible and liable persons to settle immediately the suspensions and disallowances pursuant to Section 5 of COA Circular No. 2009-006; and (b) the persons liable under ND No. 15-001-101 dated May 12, 2015 to furnish the Audit Team a copy of the appeal for reference purpose.

1.14. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a)
Municipal Treasurer (MT) to prepare a schedule of all unreleased checks every end
of the year for submission to the MA; and (b) MA to (i) make the necessary
adjustments for the restoration to the respective CIB account of the total amount of
unreleased checks of ₱51,048,589.13 to arrive at the correct balance of the account;
(ii) request the depository bank to submit the credit memos totaling ₱3,111,800.66
to support the preparation of the necessary adjusting entries in the books; and (iii)
ensure that all reconciling items discovered in the preparation of bank reconciliation
statements are adjusted to their respective accounts every end of the month.

2.12 We recommended that the LCE require the MA to (a) reclassify the Guaranty
Deposits amounting to ₱3,992,373.18 from the Receivable account as to Financial
Assets under the Investments account in accordance with the Revised Chart of
Accounts for Local Government Units (LGUs); (b) recognize impairment allowance
of ₱479,926.14 on the dormant and unsubstantiated accounts; (c) file a request for
write-off of the dormant and unsubstantiated accounts; (d) recognize Receivables
based on valid and rightful claims duly supported with adequate documentation to
present an accurate financial position of the agency; and (e) send demand letters to
the officials and employees with cash advances to immediately liquidate their
respective cash advances.

3.4. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE require the
MGSO and the MA to (a) regularly reconcile the recorded balances of Inventory
accounts with the RPCI on a monthly frequency; and (b) maintain correct, complete,
and updated SLCs and SCs to arrive at reliable and correct balances of the Inventory
accounts.

4.9. We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the LCE require the
MA and MGSO to (a) complete the physical inventory of all PPE to avail of the “One-
Time” Cleansing of PPE accounts and to reconcile the physical count per RPCPPE
with property and accounting records regularly; (b) comply with the disposition and
derecognition procedures provided for unaccounted, non-existing/missing PPEs; and
(c) MA and GSO Officer to maintain complete PPELCs, PCs and renew the ARE
every three years for control and monitoring purposes.

5.10 We recommended that the LCE require the MA to (a) analyze the composition
of the Accounts Payable account and revert to the unappropriated surplus of the
general fund the payables aging two years and more, and with no valid and sufficient
basis for their recognition as liability; (b) reclassify the unpaid claims of personnel
and employees as to Due to Officers and Employees account instead of Accounts
Payable; and (c) observe and impose appropriate cut-off dates on the submission of
reports and documents affecting the Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts to
achieve timely reconciliation of balances at year-end.

6.4. We recommended that the LCE require the MA to fast track the completion
of necessary documents to support the liquidation of unutilized fund transfers/
undisbursed funds to source agencies and/or reversion to the National Treasury in
compliance with prescribed liquidation periods.

7.6. We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to (a) reconcile the
Report of Actual Receipts of Revenues for CY 2021 with the amounts of revenues
reported in the Statement of Financial Performance; and (b) coordinate efforts to set
up the balances of Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivables at the
beginning of the year by identifying delinquent taxpayers and ensuring that RPT
registers and adequate records are properly maintained to support the balances.

8.7. We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MHRMO to submit to
the COA Audit Team the duly approved payrolls, Daily Time Records (DTRs),
Accomplishment Reports and other documentary requirements to support the
payments for hazard pay.

9.6. We recommended that the LCE in coordination with the Municipal
Development Council (MDC) (a) optimize the utilization of the DF for the planned
priority projects of the Municipality; (b) secure all necessary documents for the
planned projects before undergoing the stages of procurement; and (c) ensure
optimal utilization of the 20% DF to attain the objective of providing timely socio-
economic benefits to its constituents.

10.8. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE advise the ME
to revise and include in the contract all the general and special conditions in
accordance with the cited clauses per Sections IV and V of the Manual on Procedures
for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects, Volume III.

11.6. We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to require the Norzagaray
DepEd District Supervisors to submit to the AT the Distribution Slips for the
procured items duly acknowledged as received by the recipient schools’ principal.

12.10. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MDRRMC to (i) closely
monitor the implementation of disaster-related PPAs and (ii) maximize the use of the
5% LDRRMF through extensive planning and prudent budgetary adjustments; (b)
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly abide by the rules and procedures in
the conduct of procurement activities specifically in the submission of complete
documentary requirements as prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;
and (c) MA to submit the identified lacking documents to establish the regularity and
propriety of the disbursements.

13.10. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to maintain an SL for
Trust Liability-LDRRMF account and make the necessary adjusting entries in the
General and Trust Fund books to (i) properly record the fund transfers from the
National Government; and (ii) close the year-end balance of the QRF and MOOE to
Special Trust Fund; and (b) Local Finance Committee (LFC) to refer to Annex C of
DBM LBC No. 134 in preparing the utilization report for LGSF-DRRAP and in
complying with the relevant posting and reporting requirements.

14.10. We recommended that the LCE (a) require the BAC to strictly observe the
provisions of RA No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR by requiring the suppliers to post
warranty security to ensure that procured items are free from defects and
malfunctions; and (b) require the MDRRMO to install safeguard measures and
adequate storage/warehouse facilities to avoid pilferage, loss, and damage of DRRM
tools and equipment.

15.6 We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the LCE
require the (a) MGSO Chief to (i) insure all its property, assets and interests with the
General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System; and (ii)
prepare the Property Inventory Form (PIF) and facilitate the submission of the
updated list of insurable properties with the GSIS by following the guidelines under
Section 5.1 of COA Circular No 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 so that necessary
insurance coverage can be applied with the General Insurance Fund; and (b) Budget
Committee to include in the annual budget the insurance cost of all insurable
properties of the Municipality.

16.9 We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MGSO Chief to (i) take
responsibility over the receipt, custody and distribution of all donated goods,
supplies and equipment; (ii) prepare and maintain a Summary/List of Donations
Received, Distributed and Balances supported with acknowledgement receipts of the
donations in-kind, proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries, and
inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any; and (b) MA to account for the
inventory of the remaining relief goods in the books or in the registries and make the
necessary disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.

17.7. We recommended that the LCE require the BAC and the Municipal
Engineering Office to (a) submit the documentary requirements to the AT; (b)
comply with the posting requirements on the NTP, approved contract and the status
of project implementation consistent with the Full Disclosure Policy (FDP) of the
DILG.

18.4 We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the LCE
require the (a) MA and MT to (i) strictly comply with the regulations on the timely
rendition of accounts and financial reports to facilitate the conduct of verification
and post-audit activities; and (ii) augment the accounting personnel assigned in the
processing/filing of DVs and supporting documents to comply with the prescribed
period of submission; and (b) BAC to submit to the AT within the prescribed period
the copies of contracts, POs and delivery documents together with supporting
documents to facilitate immediate review thereof.

19.13 We recommended that the LCE as Head of the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board (a) strictly implement waste segregation at source under the
policy of No Segregation, No Collection in the household and Barangay levels; (b)
require the Inspectorate and Monitoring Team to conduct ocular inspection of
Barangay MRFs to assess their functionality, available facilities and equipment
therein; (c) encourage the SWM committees of non-compliant Barangays to propose
budgetary allocations for maintenance of composting facilities and transfer/sorting
stations in the MRFs; and (d) require the Municipal Engineer and the MENRO to
coordinate their efforts in the implementation of various capital outlay projects to
provide adequate SWM equipment, facilities, and structures especially in the
sanitary landfill.

20.7 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive require the members of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to (a)
conduct seminars and trainings through the use of technology and virtual means to
achieve the GAD-related targets amidst the new normal; (b) coordinate with the MA
for purposes of reporting and reconciling the GAD accomplishments with actual
fund disbursements; and (c) submit the GAD AR with the relevant supporting
documents such as the brief summary of the reported program or project; copies of
reported policy issuances; results of HGDG tests; and actions taken by the LGU on
the COA audit findings and recommendations.

21.6 We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the LCE require (a)
the concerned responsible and liable persons to settle immediately the suspensions
and disallowances pursuant to Section 5 of COA Circular No. 2009-006; and (b) the
persons liable under ND No. 15-001-101 dated May 12, 2015 furnish the AT with a
copy of the appeal for reference purpose.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Treasurer to prepare a schedule of all unreleased checks every end of the year for submission to the Municipal Accountant; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to   make the necessary adjustments for the restoration to the respective Cash in Bank account the total amount of unreleased checks to arrive at the correct balance of     the account.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Audit Observation No.1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) recognize transactions based on the Chart of Accounts provided under the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume III; (b) prepare the necessary correcting entries on those accounts erroneously classified; and (c) maintain and update Subsidiary Ledgers for the important accounts to prevent inaccurate and misleading information in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 2, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  that the Local Chief Executive (a)  require the Municipal Accountant to correct the misclassification of accounts by preparing an adjusting entry debiting the Advances to Special Disbursing Officers and crediting the Advances to Officers and Employees accounts; (b) refrain from granting cash advances on the last day of the year to enable the immediate liquidation of the      same; (c) advise the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update subsidiary records for all Receivables and other important accounts; and (d)  liquidate long outstanding cash advances by  sending demand letters  and strictly withholding the payment of any money due the accountable officers and employees who are still in the service, and by devising effective strategies to effect settlement thereof.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that the (a) LCE require the MGSO and the MAO to update their stock cards and supplies ledger cards, respectively, and reconcile their records regularly to arrive at a reliable inventory balances at year-end;  (b) Deputized General Services Officer to (b.i) prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventory regularly in compliance with the relevant provisions of GAM for LGUs; and (b.ii) prepare and submit to MAO the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) which shall be used as basis in preparing the JV to record the supplies and materials issued; and (c) Municipal Accountant to (c.i) restore back to the Inventory accounts the undistributed/unconsumed supplies at year-end; (c.ii) maintain a Journal of Procurements received based on the JVs drawn to recognize the supplies and materials received; and (c.iii) ensure submission of RMSI before reflecting the issuance of supplies and materials in the books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require the: (a)  the Municipal Accountant  and the General Services Officer   to reconcile their PPE records regularly; (b)  General Services Officer to (b.i) maintain and update the property cards; (b.ii) prepare and continuously update the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to serve  as  basis in dropping from the books all unserviceable property; (b.iii) issue and update the PARs for all PPE items, starting with the current year’s acquisition until all items are covered with PARs and renew/update the same at least once every three years or whenever there is a     transfer of accountability; (b.iv) exclude from the RPCPPE properties/items below the ₱15,000.00 capitalization threshold; and (b.v) desist from reporting in  the RPCPPE the  PPEs with a total amount of ₱10,801,875.00  which were not found existing during the conduct of physical inventory; (c) Municipal Accountant to (c.i) retrieve the supporting documents/records relative to the unaccounted PPE   accounts; (c.ii) derecognize  from the  PPE account  all issued tangible items  which fall below the ₱15,000.00 capitalization threshold; and (c.iii) reconcile the   discrepancy of ₱92,272,625. between the PPELCs and General Ledger by properly maintaining and updating the required Land and Land Improvement Ledger Cards and other PPE Ledger Cards for each class of PPE, and  listing therein the data as    to acquisition, description, custody, useful life and other necessary information; and (c.iv) effect the necessary adjusting entry to arrive at an accurate balance of accumulated depreciation for road network systems and remove the provision for residual value in the computation of depreciation; and  (d) Inventory Committee to (d.i) conduct regular and complete physical count of all properties of the    Municipality for CY 2021 following the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;and (d.ii) include only in the RPCPPE  the PPEs  found existing  during the physical count using  the prescribed format with  complete details on the whereabouts, condition and other relevant information relative to the PPE.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive  require (a) the Municipal Accountant, together with other concerned Offices to (i) to retrieve the documents supporting the outstanding payable accounts and settle all valid and properly documented obligations;   (b)  maintain Subsidiary Ledgers of the Liability accounts for every creditor to establish sound internal   control over these accounts and to properly monitor the transactions; and (c) set a cut-off date on the submission of reports and documents on the Due from and Due to Other Funds to effectively facilitate the reconciliation process and to reconcile the balances regularly at year-end; and (d) revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund all undocumented and long outstanding Accounts Payable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  (a) require the (i)   Municipal Treasurer to (i.a)  conduct tax mapping activities and tax campaigns in order to  achieve its collection targets for the year; (b.i.) establish the accuracy of the list of delinquent taxpayers through regular reconciliation with the registers; (ii) Land Tax Office to adequately maintain Real Property Tax Account Registers (RPTARs) to ensure the completeness and accuracy of records of taxpayers’ payments and Real Property Tax delinquencies; (iii) Municipal Accountant to  properly account for Real Property Tax Receivables in order to reflect an accurate Receivable balance in the financial statements; and (c) issue an Executive Order creating an oversight committee who shall review, set and/or adopt reasonable local fees and charges; propose a local revenue ordinance to the Sanggunian; and     institute reforms  towards enhancement of the collection performance of the Municipality thereby reducing high dependency on its IRA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) devote   attention  in the business development of its Markets and Slaughterhouse operations in order to  attain viability, profitability and prevent the incurrence of losses in the future; (b) reassign the market personnel to other departments/service units wherein their services are necessary to minimize the expenses on personal services charged to the market and slaughterhouse operations; (c) require the active participation of the MPDO and other qualified officials in the formulation of a plan or a feasibility      study for  the improvement of  Market and Slaughterhouse operations; and (d) consider utilizing the building structures to other projects that would best benefit   the Municipality’s constituents to gain more revenues.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal General Services Officer to (i)ensure all its property, assets and interests with the General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System; and (ii) to prepare the Property Inventory Form (PIF) and facilitate the submission of the updated list of insurable properties with the GSIS by following the guidelines under Section 5.1 of COA Circular No 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 so that necessary insurance coverage can be applied with the General Insurance Fund; and (b)     Budget Committee to include in the  budget the insurance expenses of   all insurable properties of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) authorized LGU representative, the DSWD Officer and other concerned officials to  (a.i)     impose stricter measures in profiling the beneficiaries specifically in times of calamities such as the conduct of  strict house to house screening; and (a.ii)  devise strategies in accordance with the requirements of DSWD MC No. 9 series of 2020;  (b) require the accountable officer to immediately liquidate the cash advances by submitting the complete supporting documents to establish the validity and   propriety of the distributed SAP; and (c) the Municipal Accounting Office to record the liquidation in the books to arrive at the correct balance of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a)  create the   Municipality’s own website to comply with the posting requirements by the DILG not only for the Bayanihan Grant but also for all procurement transactions as required by  government regulations in order to promote transparency; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to support the disbursements  with the required documentary requirements to establish  their validity and propriety.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant   to (a) advise the concerned offices to attach to the disbursement vouchers the lacking supporting documents such as authority to report to work/Office Order, and accomplishment reports; and (b) ensure sufficient documentation on disbursements in order to properly establish the validity and propriety of all Municipal government transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) in coordination with the Municipal Development Council, optimize the utilization of the DF to COVID-19 related expenses that are much needed aside from the priority projects of the Municipality; (b) Municipal Budget Officer require that  the disbursement of funds  should be within the approved allocation for each development project; (c) require the  Municipal Engineer to  stipulate the contract time in  the contract agreement; (d) Municipal Accountant to attach to the Disbursement Voucher the required documentary requirements before payment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require  the (a)      MDRRMC to (i) appropriate funds for the 5% LDRRMF based on estimated revenues from regular sources as approved in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP);    and (ii) prepare an amended LDRRMF Investment Plan and reflect in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) a separate allocation for the LDRRMF wherein the 70% Preparedness and 30% Quick Response Fund  are reflected; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i)comply with the documentary requirements provided by the CY 2016 IRR of R.A No. 9184 and other pertinent GPPB issuances;  (ii) maintain a    Trust Liability-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books as well as a subsidiary    ledger to properly account for the transfers of unexpended LDRRMF in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and (iii) make the necessary adjustments in the General and Trust Fund books in compliance with the prescribed accounting policies and procedures.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal General Service Officer  to (i) reconcile the records maintained for the donated welfare goods (ii) to  submit the correct  Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances supported with acknowledgement receipts of the    donations in-kind; proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries, not necessarily in the required form; and inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any; and (iii)  comply with the submission of the required reports to the NDRRMC through the OCD; and (b) Municipal Accountant  to account for the inventory of      the remaining relief goods in the books or in the registries and make the necessary disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to support with adequate documentation the payment of claims and allowances through completion of duly prepared DTRs, accomplishment reports, copies of contracts for CoS/JOs, and other pertinent documents to equitably     provide the Special Risk Allowance intended to the rightful personnel.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the members of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to (a) submit the Annual GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG Regional Office for approval; (b) to conduct seminars and trainings through adoption of  alternative modalities such as the use of technology and virtual means  to achieve the GAD-related targets amid the new normal; (c) coordinate with  the Municipal Accountant for purposes of  reporting  and reconciling the GAD accomplishments   with actual fund disbursements; and (d) submit the GAD AR     with the relevant supporting documents such as the brief summary of the reported program or project; copies of reported policy issuances; results of HGDG tests; and actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations.

We recommended that the   LCE require the (a) Municipal  Assessor’s Office to (i) prepare and keep an updated record of all idle lands within the Municipality; and  (ii) impose an annual tax on idle lands at the rate not exceeding five percent (5%) of the assessed value of the property in  addition to the basic real property tax; and (b) Municipal Accountant to record the Real Property Tax Receivables/Special Education Tax Receivables on Modified Accrual Basis and set up the RPT/SET every beginning of the year.
We recommended that the LCE require the concerned responsible and liable persons to settle the suspensions and disallowances immediately pursuant to Section 5 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Treasurer to prepare a schedule of all unreleased checks every end of the year for submission to the Municipal  Accountant; and (b) the Municipal  Accountant to   make the necessary adjustments for the restoration to the respective Cash in Bank account  the total amount of unreleased checks to arrive at the correct balance of     the account.
